hypothesis: Incorporating ternary metal sublattices (two distinct metal species at s-d border positions) into the chemical template framework will yield stable ternary superhydrides with non-integer hydrogen-to-metal ratios whose superconducting transition temperatures exceed those of the best binary analogues, because the electronic mismatch between the two metal species will create additional interstitial quasi-atom sites that strengthen hydrogen cage formation and increase the electron-phonon coupling constant λ beyond values achievable in single-metal clathrate structures.
